title: Star Wars: Episode II — Attack of the Clones (comic series)

'''''Star Wars: Episode II — Attack of the Clones''''' by [[Dark Horse Comics]] is the official [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] adaptation of the film [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']]. The first two issues were published on April 24, [[2002]], then issues 3 and 4 were published on May 1 and May 8, respectively.

==Plot summary==
[[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]] lands on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. However, her [[J-type diplomatic barge/Legends|ship]] and [[Cordé/Legends|decoy]] are hit with a bomb. She is, then, assigned the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|his]] [[Padawan/Legends|apprentice]] to protect her. Later, [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] sends [[Kouhun/Legends|kouhuns]] after her via [[Zam Wesell/Legends|Zam Wesell]]. Obi-Wan Kenobi jumps on [[ASN-121/Legends|ASN-121]], but Wesell shoots the droid down. Kenobi lands on a [[XJ-6 airspeeder/Legends|borrowed]] [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeder]] and they pursue the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]]. They end up at the [[Outlander Club/Legends|Outlander Club]] where a [[Balosar (species)/Legends|Balosar]] tries to sell him [[Death stick/Legends|death sticks]]. Wesell then is captured by Kenobi and Skywalker. Before she can reveal the bounty hunter's name, Fett [[Mk I Saberdart|kills]] her, her last words being ''bounty hunter slimeball'' in [[Huttese/Legends|Huttese]].

Later, Kenobi [[Jedi Archives/Legends|attempts]] to locate Fett, while Skywalker takes Amidala back to [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. Kenobi tracks Fett to [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]]. There, he discovers a [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|clone army for the Galactic Republic]], said to have been ordered by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Sifo-Dyas/Legends|Sifo-Dyas]]. He goes to Fett's apartment and is met by [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], his [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] "son."

Skywalker and Amidala had a love connection, which the latter denied. They then located [[Watto/Legends|Watto]], who told them the way to the [[Lars homestead/Legends|Lars homestead]]. Afterwards, Skywalker went to the [[Unidentified Tusken village/Legends|Tusken village]] that had [[Shmi Skywalker Lars/Legends|his mother]] and [[Mission to rescue Shmi Skywalker Lars/Legends|killed]] every one of [[Tusken Raider/Legends|them]]. He then confessed to Amidala. They then headed to [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]] to rescue Kenobi (who had [[Dogfight over Geonosis/Legends|tracked Fett]] and was captured), which [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|broke]] into a [[Clone Wars/Legends|full-scale war]], in which [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] almost defeated [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] (or Darth Tyranus), who fled.

In the end, Count Dooku returned to to his [[Sith Master/Legends|Master]] [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Sidious]] to report that "The war has begun." Then, Yoda, [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]], and Kenobi are talking, and Yoda announces, "The shroud of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] has fallen. Begun [[Clone Wars/Legends|this Clone War]] has." Later, [[Bail Prestor Organa/Legends|Bail Prestor Organa]], [[Onaconda Farr/Legends|Onaconda Farr]], [[Mas Amedda/Legends|Mas Amedda]], and Palpatine are watching as [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] load on ships. On Naboo, Skywalker and Amidala kiss in a [[Wedding of Anakin Skywalker and Padmé Amidala/Legends|holy marriage]].

==Development==
Writer [[Henry Gilroy]] had completed his work on the adaption by the time Director [[George Lucas]] added the [[Geonosis droid factories/Legends|droid factory]] sequence into the film, much to Gilroy's surprise. As it was added so late in development, Gilroy was told he could only fit the sequence into one page.<ref name="FullSith">{{WebCite|url=https://fullofsith.libsyn.com/fos/episode-dciii-the-legacy-of-ilm-skywalker-sound-and-lucasfilm-animation|text=Episode DCIII: The Legacy of ILM, Skywalker Sound, and Lucasfilm Animation|author=[[Full of Sith Podcast]]|date=2025-09-16|archivedate=20260125031235}}</ref>
